title: Your favorite barista might be a paid TikTok creator now, thanks to Starbucks
tldr: Starbucks is paying its own baristas to create TikTok content, turning frontline employees into brand creators. A concrete, scaled example of employee-generated content as a marketing channel.
angle: How brands are formalizing employee-generated content into paid creator programs — and what governance that requires.

title: Content Creator Jobs: What It Takes to Work in Food, Makeup, and Gaming
tldr: Breaks down how niche shapes the working life of professional creators as the creator economy heads toward $500B by 2030. Useful map of how content work is professionalizing.
angle: What the professionalization of creator jobs means for brands hiring or partnering with niche creators.

title: The Scoop: The secret propaganda push capitalizing on anti-data center sentiment
tldr: A covert influence campaign is exploiting the fact that 71% of Americans don't want data centers nearby. A vivid case in issue management and astroturfed narratives.
angle: How the AI-infrastructure backlash is becoming a communications battleground brands must navigate.

title: JAIKU founder Marianne Olaleye on why we shouldn't obsess with Gen Z, and how storytelling can change the world
tldr: Argues against demographic obsession and for universal, human-centered storytelling. A counter-narrative to audience-segmentation orthodoxy.
angle: Why chasing generational cohorts weakens storytelling — and what human-first narrative looks like instead.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues that pitching AI as productivity gains fails with CMOs, CFOs and legal, and offers audience-specific reframes to unlock budget. Practical internal-comms and positioning advice.
angle: How to reframe AI's value per stakeholder instead of leaning on the tired 'productivity' story.

title: How to track your brand's presence in AI search
tldr: Lays out the new metrics of answer-engine optimization — mentions, citations, share of voice — as AI results replace traditional SERPs. Timely for measuring visibility.
angle: Building a share-of-voice framework for the AI-answer era beyond classic SEO.

title: Study: Google's Sandbox won't save publisher profits
tldr: Google's own experiment shows Privacy Sandbox fails to close the revenue gap for publishers. A structural blow to publisher monetization models.
angle: What the failure of Privacy Sandbox means for owned-media and publisher business models.

title: Vitaminwater doubles down on content series celebrating local art
tldr: Season two of Neighborhue expands to a new city with documentary video, long-form editorial and social-first content celebrating local artists. A clean branded-content case study.
angle: Breaking down how a brand builds a multi-format, human-centered content series around local culture.

title: The social listening metrics every PR pro should track
tldr: Details which social-listening metrics actually surface audience hopes and fears, plus a caution on over-relying on them. Grounded guidance for sentiment and monitoring.
angle: Which social-listening signals genuinely predict reputation risk versus vanity noise.

title: Как разные страны вводят квоты в кинотеатрах и на телевидении
tldr: A critic examines film-quota policy in ten very different countries and finds no one has cracked the formula in a century. Rich material on media policy and cultural-content strategy.
angle: What a century of failed content quotas teaches about protecting local storytelling ecosystems.

title: The Boston Globe returns its website to yesteryear for American independence
tldr: For the Semiquincentennial the Globe reimagined its homepage as it might have looked on July 4, 1776. A playful design/editorial stunt worth studying.
angle: How playful format experiments can renew audience attention for legacy publishers.

title: SKYN 'Heat Map' Tracks Fans' Chemistry and Action No Broadcaster Covers
tldr: SKYN created a real-time 'Heat Map' capturing fan chemistry that broadcasters ignore, turning a brand into a data-driven content producer. A fresh data-storytelling campaign.
angle: How brands fill gaps left by broadcasters with their own real-time data content.

title: No sweat: How Unilever kept its brands fresh on consumers' minds during World Cup
tldr: A breakdown of Unilever's always-on brand presence strategy through the World Cup. Concrete campaign mechanics for staying top-of-mind during a mega-event.
angle: What sustained-presence tactics during tentpole events teach about editorial planning cadence.

title: Social Media Drives 65% of Mexico Consumer Purchases: Kueski
tldr: New data shows social media now drives 65% of consumer purchases in Mexico, a striking shift in media-consumption and buying behavior. Useful audience-behavior signal.
angle: How social-first purchasing behavior in emerging markets reshapes content and commerce strategy.
